name: Phở bò (Beef Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Lightly sweet from simmered bones, deeply savory, fragrant with warm raw spices
ai_search_tags: breakfast, winter, comforting, traditional, national dish, hangover cure
detailed_description: Phở bò is the crown jewel of Hanoi's cuisine, built on a crystal-clear broth simmered for hours from beef leg bones. Tender, chewy rice noodle strands pair with thinly sliced beef and fresh coriander to create a flavor that wins over every diner.

name: Phở gà (Chicken Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Light and refreshing, crystal-clear broth, richly savory from free-range chicken
ai_search_tags: breakfast, light meal, delicate, traditional, easy to digest, lime leaf
detailed_description: Phở gà charms diners with a sweet broth simmered from chicken bones, topped with a thin golden layer of fat gently floating on the surface. The free-range chicken is firm and chewy, sliced into bite-sized pieces and finished with a sprinkle of fragrant shredded lime leaf in true Hanoi style.

name: Bún ốc nguội (Cold Snail Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Gently sour, fiery spicy, cool and refreshing, crunchy
ai_search_tags: summer, refreshing, snack, sour and spicy, cooling, street stroll
detailed_description: This dish carries the nostalgic charm of old Hanoi, served with a cold broth that's mildly sour from fermented rice vinegar and fiery from chili oil. Each plump, crunchy stuffed snail is dipped and eaten with cool loose vermicelli, perfect for beating the summer heat.

name: Bún ốc nóng (Hot Snail Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Rich and savory, mildly sour, deeply fragrant with perilla and fermented rice vinegar
ai_search_tags: winter, comforting, breakfast, gently sour, picnic, gathering with friends
detailed_description: Bún ốc nóng is irresistible with its bubbling hot bowl of broth, mildly soured with tomato and fermented sticky rice vinegar. It's served with snails boiled just right, topped with finely chopped perilla and scallion for a warm, harmonious finish.

name: Bún riêu cua (Crab Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Sweet from field crab, gently sour, fragrant with fermented shrimp paste
ai_search_tags: breakfast, refreshing, homestyle, savory, afternoon snack, easy to eat
detailed_description: A proper bowl of bún riêu stands out with its shimmering crab roe topping, a gently sour broth, and golden fried tofu. To bring out its signature flavor, a touch of pure fermented shrimp paste is the essential seasoning for true connoisseurs.

name: Bún thang (Hanoi Mixed Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Elegant and delicate, gently sweet, intricate and refined
ai_search_tags: refined, serene, festive occasions, special, breakfast, elegant
detailed_description: Known as the 'flower' of Hanoi cuisine, bún thang demands meticulous care in finely shredding every ingredient. The broth must be perfectly clear, sweetened naturally by chicken bones and dried shrimp, and finished with a touch of rare cà cuống essence for a truly special aroma.

name: Bún cá (Fish Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Sweet and clean fish flavor, crunchy, gently sour
ai_search_tags: lunch, change of pace, savory, winter, everyday, fragrant
detailed_description: Hanoi's bún cá stands out with richly seasoned fish slices fried to a crisp golden brown without turning mushy. The broth, simmered from fish bones, is naturally sweet and pairs beautifully with crunchy water celery or fresh mustard greens.

name: Bún dọc mùng (Taro Stem Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Refreshing, crunchy taro stem, richly savory pork knuckle
ai_search_tags: refreshing, lunch, hearty, familiar, easy to digest, crunchy
detailed_description: The highlight of this dish is the carefully peeled taro stem, rubbed with salt to remove its itch, giving a satisfying crunch with every bite. Paired with tender, richly savory pork knuckle, this noodle dish easily wins over even the pickiest eaters.

name: Bún mọc (Pork Meatball Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Lightly sweet, rustic, fragrant with shiitake mushroom
ai_search_tags: breakfast, gentle, family, homestyle, easy to eat, comforting
detailed_description: A simple yet refined noodle dish featuring springy meatballs made from pounded pork paste mixed with finely chopped shiitake mushroom and wood ear fungus. The crystal-clear broth, simmered from baby pork ribs, is rounded out with a few slices of cinnamon pork sausage for an irresistible, rustic flavor.

name: Bún ngan (Muscovy Duck Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Rich and savory, deeply fatty, crunchy bamboo shoots, fragrant with Vietnamese coriander
ai_search_tags: lunch, drinking food, late night, gathering, savory, filling
detailed_description: Tender, juicy boiled muscovy duck is the soul of this noodle bowl. What sets it apart is the naturally sweet broth released during boiling, served with simmered dried bamboo shoots that soak up an irresistibly rich flavor.

name: Phở sốt vang (Red Wine Beef Stew Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Rich and savory, deeply fatty, hints of red wine and five-spice powder
ai_search_tags: winter, breakfast, comforting, savory, late night, nourishing
detailed_description: Phở sốt vang is a unique fusion of French and Vietnamese cuisine. Beef brisket is slow-braised with red wine and gấc fruit, creating an eye-catching reddish-orange hue and a rich flavor that's perfect for Hanoi's chilly, windy days.

name: Miến lươn nước (Eel Glass Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Deeply sweet with eel flavor, fragrant with Vietnamese coriander, eel served crispy or tender
ai_search_tags: lunch, refreshing, specialty, nourishing, Vietnamese coriander, change of pace
detailed_description: Miến lươn nước wins diners over with its richly sweet broth, made entirely from finely pounded eel bones. The chewy glass noodles pair with crispy fried eel or tender stir-fried eel, finished with fragrant Vietnamese coriander.

name: Miến gà (Chicken Glass Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Light and sweet, gentle, fragrant with mushroom
ai_search_tags: breakfast, light meal, delicate, easy to digest, comforting, traditional
detailed_description: A proper bowl of miến gà offers a light, refreshing feel with a clear, low-fat chicken broth. Chewy glass noodles combine with shredded free-range chicken and crunchy wood ear and shiitake mushrooms for a perfectly light breakfast.

name: Mì vằn thắn (Wonton Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Richly sweet with dried shrimp flavor, chewy noodles, rich wontons
ai_search_tags: dinner, hearty, filling, savory, old quarter, family
detailed_description: Hanoi's mì vằn thắn features a sweet broth simmered carefully from leg bones and dried shrimp shells. The hearty bowl comes loaded with golden fresh egg noodles, melt-in-your-mouth wontons, slices of char siu, pork liver, and bright green chives for a wonderful blend of flavors.

name: Bún sườn sụn (Cartilage Rib Noodle Soup)
taste_profile: Gently sour, crunchy cartilage ribs, broth sweet from bones
ai_search_tags: breakfast, lunch, crunchy, homestyle, easy to eat, comforting
detailed_description: This addictive dish features cartilage ribs simmered just right, retaining their signature crunch with every bite. A mildly sour broth from tomato, paired with refreshing taro stem, makes for an incredibly moreish bowl of noodles.

name: Nem chua rán (Fried Fermented Pork Rolls)
taste_profile: Richly fatty, crispy outside, chewy and tender inside, lightly sweet
ai_search_tags: snack, street food, students, gathering, late afternoon, sweet and spicy
detailed_description: Nem chua rán is an iconic snack that's a must for Hanoi's younger crowd. Each fermented pork roll is rolled in breadcrumbs and deep-fried until golden, then dipped in a sweet-spicy chili sauce for a rich, irresistible flavor.

name: Bánh tôm Hồ Tây (West Lake Shrimp Cakes)
taste_profile: Crispy, sweet from fresh shrimp, harmoniously sweet and sour
ai_search_tags: late afternoon, stroll, West Lake, gathering, crispy, specialty
detailed_description: Tied closely to the scenic West Lake, this dish stands out with its batter of wheat flour mixed with shredded sweet potato, fried to a satisfying crisp. On top sits a whole shell-on shrimp, rolled with fresh herbs and dipped in green papaya fish sauce to cut the richness.

name: Bánh gối (Vietnamese Pillow Dumplings)
taste_profile: Crispy shell, deeply savory filling fragrant with pepper, rich
ai_search_tags: afternoon snack, winter, street food, students, comforting, crispy
detailed_description: Shaped like a small pillow, this fried wheat-flour pastry turns golden brown and crispy on the outside. Inside is a savory mix of minced pork, glass noodles, wood ear fungus, and a single quail egg, all served with a sweet-and-sour dipping sauce.

name: Bánh rán mặn (Savory Fried Rice Cakes)
taste_profile: Chewy yet crispy sticky rice shell, savory pork filling, sweet and spicy sauce
ai_search_tags: winter, afternoon snack, street food, chewy, comforting, budget-friendly
detailed_description: Unlike its sweet counterpart, this savory rice cake has a chewy sticky-rice shell that's fried crisp on the outside, wrapped around a fragrant filling of minced pork and wood ear fungus. It's typically cut up into a bowl and drizzled with a thick, sweet-spicy-savory sauce.

name: Bánh rán ngọt (Sweet Fried Rice Cakes)
taste_profile: Delightfully sweet, fragrant with sesame, crispy golden shell
ai_search_tags: snack, afternoon treat, sweet tooth, street food, students, nostalgic
detailed_description: Bánh rán ngọt is woven into the childhood memories of countless Hanoians, with two popular variations: sugar-glazed and molasses-glazed. The crispy sesame-coated shell wraps around a sweet, fluffy mung bean and coconut filling.

name: Bánh đúc nóng (Hot Savory Rice Cake)
taste_profile: Silky and soft, richly fatty from scallion oil, deeply savory minced pork
ai_search_tags: winter, late afternoon, comforting, chewy, students, light meal
detailed_description: Bánh đúc nóng is the perfect afternoon treat for Hanoi's cold days. The chewy, steaming rice cake is topped with savory stir-fried minced pork and wood ear fungus, then drenched in a mildly sweet-and-sour fish sauce.

name: Nộm bò khô (Dried Beef Salad)
taste_profile: Crunchy, sweet and sour, fiery spicy, nutty from peanuts
ai_search_tags: afternoon snack, stroll, old quarter, refreshing, casual drinking, sweet and sour
detailed_description: A famous street food on Hoan Kiem's streets, this salad features thinly shaved, crunchy green papaya. Its soul lies in the spicy marinated dried beef and rich dried liver, tossed together with sweet-and-sour fish sauce and fragrant roasted peanuts.

name: Bánh giò (Pyramid-Shaped Rice Dumpling)
taste_profile: Soft, slightly springy wrapper, richly fatty filling, hint of banana leaf aroma
ai_search_tags: breakfast, afternoon snack, soft texture, comforting, traditional, springy
detailed_description: This familiar pyramid-shaped cake is carefully wrapped in fresh green banana leaf. Once unwrapped, the soft, slightly springy rice dough reveals a richly savory minced pork and wood ear fungus filling with an irresistible aroma.

name: Lòng rán (Fried Pork Offal)
taste_profile: Crispy outside, chewy inside, richly fatty
ai_search_tags: drinks with friends, casual drinking, gathering, evening, crispy, everyday
detailed_description: Cleaned and richly marinated pork intestine and stomach are deep-fried until golden brown. Served alongside rice noodle sheets and dipped in whipped fermented shrimp paste with fragrant Thai basil, it's the perfect bite for an evening hangout.

name: Dồi sụn nướng (Grilled Cartilage Sausage)
taste_profile: Deeply aromatic, satisfyingly crunchy cartilage, richly marinated
ai_search_tags: snack, street food, fragrant, crunchy, casual drinking, late afternoon
detailed_description: Plump sausage links sizzle over a charcoal grill, filling the street corner with an irresistible aroma. The chewy casing holds a crunchy cartilage filling mixed with minced pork and fresh herbs for a fun, satisfying chew.

name: Ốc luộc (Boiled Snails)
taste_profile: Crunchy, fatty snails, salty-sweet fish sauce with fiery lemongrass and ginger
ai_search_tags: winter, hanging out, chatting, fiery spicy, gathering, street food
detailed_description: Street-side snail stalls are a beloved part of Hanoi's food culture. Fresh snails are boiled just right with lime leaves and lemongrass, then picked out and dipped into a perfectly thick, smooth ginger-chili fish sauce.

name: Nem chua nướng (Grilled Fermented Pork Rolls)
taste_profile: Fragrant golden-charred shell, chewy and sticky, blend of sour, spicy, salty, and sweet
ai_search_tags: afternoon snack, winter, hanging out, sticky, casual stroll, comforting
detailed_description: Unlike its deep-fried counterpart, nem chua here is grilled directly over hot charcoal until it renders fat and chars at the edges. The result is a sticky, chewy texture with a gently fermented sourness and a smoky aroma that's deeply addictive.

name: Chim quay (Roasted Quail)
taste_profile: Glossy crispy skin, tender sweet meat soaked in spices, fragrant with mắc khén pepper
ai_search_tags: dinner, drinking food, casual drinking, savory, street food, distinctive
detailed_description: Quail marinated in five-spice powder and honey are roasted to a glossy golden brown. Biting in, the crispy skin shatters to reveal tender, sweet meat underneath, best enjoyed with a chili-lime salt-and-pepper dip.

name: Bò pía ngọt (Sweet Coconut Rice Paper Rolls)
taste_profile: Thin chewy wrapper, crispy sweet malt candy, richly fatty fresh coconut
ai_search_tags: childhood, school gate, students, sweet, walking snack, nostalgic
detailed_description: This humble treat is closely tied to the bicycle vendors who once sold it outside school gates. A thin rice paper wrapper rolls up a crispy stick of malt candy, with a sprinkle of grated coconut and black sesame for a sweet taste of childhood.

name: Bánh mì dân tổ (Loaded Street Sandwich)
taste_profile: Richly fatty mixed filling stir-fried together, crispy baguette shell, deeply savory
ai_search_tags: late night, night owls, queuing, rich, mixed filling, young crowd
detailed_description: Famous as the go-to food for Hanoi's night owls, this sandwich's standout feature is its all-in-one filling. Egg, pâté, sausage, and ham are all stir-fried together in one large pan into a rich, gooey mixture, then stuffed into a crispy baguette.

name: Bít tết Hòe Nhai (Hòe Nhai Street Steak)
taste_profile: Richly fatty from pâté, sizzling tender sweet beef, deeply savory sauce
ai_search_tags: dinner, casually upscale, date night, lively, sizzling, filling
detailed_description: Unlike Western-style steak, Hanoi's street steak is served on a sizzling hot cast-iron pan still bubbling with fat. A generous portion comes with richly marinated beef, a runny sunny-side-up egg, and rich pâté, all paired with a sauce for dipping bread.

name: Phở chiên phồng (Puffed Fried Phở Noodles)
taste_profile: Puffed, crispy phở noodles, tender garlic-fried beef, smooth thick sauce
ai_search_tags: dinner, gathering, crispy, savory, unique flavor, Ngu Xa
detailed_description: A specialty of the Ngu Xa neighborhood, this dish features square-cut rice noodle sheets deep-fried until they puff up and turn delightfully crispy. They're topped with garlic-fried beef and bright green choy sum, all drenched in a smooth, deeply savory sauce.

name: Trứng vịt lộn tần ngải cứu (Balut Soup with Mugwort)
taste_profile: Deeply savory with Chinese herbal notes, slight bitterness from mugwort, rich and creamy egg
ai_search_tags: late night, nourishing, comforting, winter, cold remedy, everyday
detailed_description: A late-night dish that's both humble and nourishing for Hanoians. Balut is slow-simmered with young mugwort leaves and Chinese herbal spices, producing a dark, slightly bitter broth with a surprisingly sweet and warming aftertaste.

name: Quẩy nóng (Hot Fried Dough Sticks)
taste_profile: Crispy and airy outside, hollow inside, harmoniously sweet and sour
ai_search_tags: winter, snack, late afternoon, crispy, street food, students
detailed_description: Whenever the cool season rolls around, hot quẩy stalls get busy with customers. Strips of dough are dropped into bubbling hot oil, puffing up airy and golden, then cut up and dipped into a sweet-and-sour green papaya fish sauce.

name: Thịt xiên nướng (Grilled Pork Skewers)
taste_profile: Deeply fragrant with lemongrass, charred glossy fat, sweet from honey
ai_search_tags: school gate, after work, fragrant, snack, street stroll, casual drinking
detailed_description: Any school gate or street corner around closing time will have the faint, irresistible smoke of grilled skewers drifting through the air. Pork shoulder marinated in lemongrass, shallots, and honey is grilled until charred at the edges, delicious eaten on its own or tucked into a baguette.

name: Cà phê trứng (Egg Coffee)
taste_profile: Richly fatty, airy and smooth egg cream, lightly bitter coffee, no fishy taste at all
ai_search_tags: date night, old quarter, morning, sipping slowly, specialty, richly fatty
detailed_description: One of Hanoi's most creative drinks, featuring a smooth, airy whipped egg cream floating atop a base of strong black coffee. The secret lies in the delicate treatment of the egg yolk, resulting in a rich, sweet cup with absolutely no eggy aftertaste.

name: Nước sấu đá (Iced Sấu Fruit Drink)
taste_profile: Gently sour, refreshingly sweet, hint of mildly spicy ginger
ai_search_tags: summer, refreshing drink, stroll, cooling, street food, childhood
detailed_description: This humble drink fully captures the essence of a Hanoi summer. Crunchy sấu fruit is soaked in sugar, and the resulting syrup is mixed with cold ice and a few slices of ginger for instant refreshment.

name: Trà chanh (Lemon Tea)
taste_profile: Lightly astringent tea, harmoniously sweet and sour, fragrant with lime zest oils
ai_search_tags: hanging out, gathering, street food, cathedral area, cooling, evening
detailed_description: The 'lemon tea and idle chat' culture has become an intangible specialty of Hanoi's youth. The flavor is simple, just a blend of lightly astringent black tea, the gentle sourness of fresh lime, and mild sweetness, making it a beloved national refreshment.

name: Trà đá vỉa hè (Street-Side Iced Tea)
taste_profile: Sharply astringent upfront, refined sweet finish, cool and refreshing
ai_search_tags: everyday, after meals, hanging out, chatting, refreshing, budget-friendly
detailed_description: Rain or shine, the iced tea stalls under the shade of almond trees are always bustling. A glass of lightly brewed tea with a few clear ice cubes is all it takes to kick off countless everyday conversations among the city's locals.

name: Chè sen nhãn lồng (Lotus Seed and Longan Sweet Soup)
taste_profile: Lightly sweet from rock sugar, crunchy longan, soft mealy lotus seeds
ai_search_tags: summer, refined, cooling, nourishing, traditional, soothing
detailed_description: This elegant sweet soup showcases the skill of Hanoi's home cooks. Soft, mealy lotus seeds are nestled inside thick, juicy longan flesh, then served in a clear rock-sugar syrup infused with jasmine for a gently sweet finish.

name: Chè khúc bạch (Almond Jelly Sweet Soup)
taste_profile: Gently sweet, wobbly milk jelly that melts in the mouth, nutty toasted almonds
ai_search_tags: cooling, summer, trendy, refreshing, dessert, sweet tooth
detailed_description: Once a major youth trend, chè khúc bạch wins hearts with its soft, wobbly milk-and-cheese jelly cubes. The bowl is even more inviting with cooling rock-sugar syrup, crunchy longan flesh, and a layer of fragrant toasted sliced almonds.

name: Tào phớ hoa nhài (Jasmine Tofu Pudding)
taste_profile: Silky smooth on the throat, lightly sweet, fragrant with jasmine
ai_search_tags: summer, refreshing, gentle, snack, street stroll, traditional
detailed_description: The street call of 'Tofu pudding here...' once echoed familiarly through the city. Thin, snow-white slices of tofu pudding are drenched in an amber-colored apricot blossom syrup, infused with pure jasmine fragrance to wash away the day's exhaustion.

name: Sữa chua dẻo (Chewy Yogurt)
taste_profile: Gently tangy and sweet, chewy and smooth, lightly bitter cocoa
ai_search_tags: dessert, afternoon snack, date night, refreshing, easy to digest, stroll
detailed_description: Neither as runny as regular yogurt nor as firm as ice, chewy yogurt has a dense, jelly-like texture and is cut into neat squares. It's usually finished with a light dusting of cocoa powder or a colorful sprinkle of diced fresh fruit.

name: Kem Tràng Tiền (Tràng Tiền Ice Cream)
taste_profile: Delightfully sweet, fragrant and chewy with cốm flavor, nutty mung bean, refreshingly cold
ai_search_tags: walking, Hoan Kiem Lake stroll, summer, nostalgic, eating standing up, childhood
detailed_description: Visiting the lake without standing on the street to eat a Tràng Tiền ice cream cone simply doesn't count as visiting Hanoi. With no tables or chairs, customers stand shoulder to shoulder enjoying the fragrant cốm or mung bean flavors, a unique cultural ritual found nowhere else.

name: Cà phê cốt dừa (Coconut Coffee)
taste_profile: Sharp bitter coffee blending with rich, refreshingly cool coconut
ai_search_tags: date night, hanging out, richly fatty, cooling, energizing, work breaks
detailed_description: A modern twist beloved by the younger crowd. A snowy-white blended coconut cream and condensed milk layer is slowly poured over a base of dark drip coffee, creating a striking marbled effect and an addictive bitter-rich flavor combo.

name: Cốm làng Vòng (Vong Village Young Rice)
taste_profile: Gently sweet, fragrant and chewy with the scent of young rice, hint of refined lotus leaf aroma
ai_search_tags: autumn, gift item, refined, traditional, sipping slowly, afternoon tea
detailed_description: This treat encapsulates Hanoi's autumn in a single package. Flat, vivid green grains of young rice are hand-pounded with meticulous care, then carefully wrapped in two layers — a wild taro leaf to retain moisture and a fragrant lotus leaf — tied with golden straw cord.

name: Ô mai (Vietnamese Salted Dried Fruit)
taste_profile: Harmoniously sour, salty, and sweet, warming spicy notes from ginger
ai_search_tags: gift item, snack, sipping slowly, Hang Duong Street, traditional, sweet and sour
detailed_description: Hang Duong Street is famous for its intricately packaged boxes of dried fruit. Countless varieties of fruit are skillfully preserved with sugar, ginger, and licorice, then carefully dried to create a sweet-and-sour treat that awakens every sense.

name: Trà sen Tây Hồ (West Lake Lotus Tea)
ingredients: West Lake Bach Diep lotus flower
taste_profile: Gently astringent and refined, deep sweet finish, pure lotus fragrance
ai_search_tags: gift giving, premium, serene, tea appreciation, elegant, specialty
detailed_description: Dubbed the 'finest tea under heaven,' this requires artisans to use thousands of Bach Diep lotus blossoms picked at dawn to scent just a small batch of tea. The lotus fragrance infuses every leaf, resulting in a noble, serene flavor.

name: Bánh cốm (Young Rice Cake)
taste_profile: Chewy green young-rice shell, sweet and nutty mung bean filling, hint of pomelo flower aroma
ai_search_tags: gift item, weddings, traditional, Hang Than Street, sweet, chewy
detailed_description: An essential gift in Northern Vietnamese wedding ceremonies. The jade-green, chewy young-rice shell wraps around a sweet, nutty mung bean and coconut filling, lightly scented with delicate pomelo flower water.

name: Bánh chả (Grilled Pork Pastry)
taste_profile: Crispy shell, richly fatty pork fat, fragrant lime leaf
ai_search_tags: afternoon tea, winter, gift item, richly fatty, traditional, sipping slowly
detailed_description: This small baked pastry holds a world of memories. A crispy wheat-flour shell wraps around a filling of diced pork fat mixed with Vietnamese sausage and lime leaf, rich and deeply satisfying alongside a hot cup of black tea.

name: Bánh khảo (Roasted Rice Flour Cake)
taste_profile: Distinctly sweet, fine and airy melting texture, richly fatty pork fat
ai_search_tags: Lunar New Year, traditional, gift item, sipping slowly, green tea, festive feast
detailed_description: A humble cake made from pure roasted sticky rice flour. The fine, airy, sweet shell wraps around a filling of candied winter melon and rich pork fat, melting on the tongue with a lingering hint of pomelo flower fragrance.

name: Giò chả Ước Lễ (Ước Lễ Pork Sausage)
taste_profile: Fragrant with boiled banana leaf, satisfyingly springy, sweet from fresh meat
ai_search_tags: Lunar New Year, gift giving, family, festive feast, traditional, deeply savory
detailed_description: A long-established, famous pork sausage brand in the capital. Each slice has a light pink color with tiny air pockets (a sign of no added borax), offering a satisfying spring and a natural sweetness from hand-pounded fresh meat.

name: Chè lam (Sticky Rice and Molasses Candy)
taste_profile: Deeply sweet from molasses, warmly spicy ginger, chewy and nutty
ai_search_tags: winter, comforting, sipping slowly, gift item, afternoon tea, rustic
detailed_description: Chè lam is a warming, rustic treat for cold winter days. The chewy bar has a glossy brown hue from sugarcane molasses, studded with nutty roasted peanuts and carrying the signature warming spice of fresh ginger.

name: Sấu ngâm (Pickled Sấu Fruit)
taste_profile: Sour and sweet, crunchy sấu fruit that doesn't go soft
ai_search_tags: summer, refreshing drink, homemade, gift item, sweet and sour, cooling
detailed_description: Jars of pickled sấu fruit in sugar syrup are a familiar sight in Hanoi kitchens every summer. The fruit is peeled and scored, then pickled in sugar and ginger while retaining its signature crunch, making for a wonderfully thirst-quenching iced drink.

name: Bánh phu thê (Husband and Wife Cake)
taste_profile: Chewy, springy shell with coconut crunch, sweet and nutty mung bean filling
ai_search_tags: weddings, gift item, Hang Than Street, sweet and nutty, chewy, symbolic of happiness
detailed_description: A symbolic cake representing loyalty and marital fulfillment at weddings. The glossy golden, translucent, chewy shell reveals strands of crunchy shredded coconut, wrapped around a refined, sweet mung bean and lotus seed filling.

name: Chả cá Lã Vọng (Lã Vọng Grilled Fish)
taste_profile: Fragrant grilled fish with fermented rice and galangal, richly fatty, infused with fermented shrimp paste
ai_search_tags: hosting guests, specialty, weekend, savory, upscale, family
detailed_description: One of Hanoi's most famous dishes, named directly after the street where it originated. Lăng fish marinated in galangal and fermented rice is grilled to a golden char, then stir-fried tableside in bubbling oil with scallion and dill, served with rice noodles and nutty roasted peanuts.

name: Bún chả (Grilled Pork with Vermicelli)
taste_profile: Smoky charred grilled pork, harmoniously sweet and sour dipping sauce, refreshing
ai_search_tags: lunch, national dish, savory, filling, famous, traditional
detailed_description: A beloved national dish that has captured the hearts of countless diners. Sliced pork belly and minced pork patties are grilled over charcoal until smoky and fragrant, then soaked in a warm, sweet-and-sour fish sauce dressed with crunchy green papaya.

name: Bún đậu mắm tôm (Vermicelli with Tofu and Shrimp Paste)
taste_profile: Crispy outside, soft inside tofu, deeply savory and rich fermented shrimp paste
ai_search_tags: lunch, hanging out, distinctive, savory, gathering, street food
detailed_description: A bold yet perfectly balanced combination from Vietnamese street food culture. A generous tray comes with golden fried tofu, thinly sliced boiled pork, and fragrant young-rice sausage, all dipped into a frothy whipped fermented shrimp paste with kumquat and sizzling hot oil.

name: Cơm rang dưa bò (Fried Rice with Beef and Pickled Mustard)
taste_profile: Light and fluffy rice glossed with oil, tender beef, crunchy pickled mustard cuts the richness
ai_search_tags: filling, lunch, savory, everyday, sizzling, office workers
detailed_description: An ideal quick fix for a rushed lunch. The rice is fried light and fluffy, tinted golden by egg, and served with a side of richly savory beef stir-fried with pickled mustard, fragrant with garlic and soy sauce.

name: Xôi xéo (Turmeric Sticky Rice with Mung Bean)
taste_profile: Chewy and dense rice, nutty rich mung bean, crispy fried shallots, fatty oil
ai_search_tags: breakfast, filling, quick bite, budget-friendly, traditional, chewy and fragrant
detailed_description: A simple breakfast treat with a beautiful golden hue from turmeric. A handful of dense, chewy sticky rice is topped with thin slices of pressed mung bean, finished with crispy fried shallots and a generous drizzle of rich pork fat oil.

name: Xôi khúc (Mugwort-Wrapped Sticky Rice Dumpling)
taste_profile: Soft chewy sticky rice, deeply fragrant with khúc leaf and pepper, richly fatty filling
ai_search_tags: winter, late night, street vendor calls, richly fatty, comforting, nostalgic
detailed_description: The street call of 'Xôi khúc here...' echoing through narrow alleys is a familiar sound of Hanoi winters. The round sticky rice dumpling encases a fragrant dark-green khúc-leaf dough, with a rich pork and mung bean and pepper filling inside.

name: Bánh cuốn Thanh Trì (Thanh Trì Steamed Rice Rolls)
taste_profile: Paper-thin sheets that slide smoothly, gently sweet fish sauce, deeply savory cinnamon sausage
ai_search_tags: breakfast, refreshing, traditional, delicate, refined, light meal
detailed_description: A renowned specialty of Thanh Trì village, known for its technique of rolling sheets as thin as paper. The rolls have no minced meat filling, just a touch of scallion oil and finely chopped wood ear fungus, served with slices of fragrant cinnamon sausage and a mildly sweet fish sauce.

name: Phở cuốn (Phở Noodle Rolls)
taste_profile: Light and refreshing, cool, deeply savory stir-fried beef, fresh crunchy raw vegetables
ai_search_tags: lunch, summer, light, change of pace, Ngu Xa, not heavy
detailed_description: A relative latecomer that has quickly become a favorite light bite. A large sheet of rice noodle is rolled around hot, garlicky stir-fried beef and a generous mix of fresh greens, dipped in a sweet-and-sour fish sauce that perfectly cuts through any richness.

name: Nem rán (Chả giò) (Fried Spring Rolls)
taste_profile: Crispy shell, richly fatty mixed filling with natural sweetness, deeply savory
ai_search_tags: festive feast, family, weekend, crispy, traditional, national dish
detailed_description: A staple dish that's never absent from any Lunar New Year feast. The intricacy lies in mixing dozens of ingredients for the filling and rolling them tightly, then deep-frying in plenty of oil until the wrapper turns a crisp, eye-catching amber color.

name: Cháo sườn sụn (Cartilage Rib Rice Porridge)
taste_profile: Cream-smooth porridge, crunchy cartilage, salty pork floss, lightly spicy
ai_search_tags: late afternoon, breakfast, comforting, smooth, kid-friendly, winter
detailed_description: Unlike regular whole-grain porridge, Hanoi-style cháo sườn is made from finely ground rice flour, resulting in a thick, smooth, almost cream-like texture. A spoonful with tender, crunchy cartilage rib, topped with crispy fried dough sticks and savory pork floss, is the ultimate comfort food.

name: Bún bò Nam Bộ (Southern-Style Beef Vermicelli Salad)
taste_profile: Refreshingly sweet and sour, tender deeply savory beef, nutty roasted peanuts
ai_search_tags: lunch, refreshing, summer, filling, not heavy, easy to eat
detailed_description: Despite its Southern name, this mixed noodle dish is hugely popular on the capital's streets. The bowl is a harmony of colors and flavors: cool vermicelli, hot stir-fried beef, crunchy refreshing bean sprouts, all tossed in a gently sweet-and-sour fish sauce.

name: Xôi thịt kho trứng (Sticky Rice with Braised Pork and Egg)
taste_profile: Dense and chewy, fall-apart braised pork rendering fat, rich pâté
ai_search_tags: breakfast, long-lasting fullness, savory, richly fatty, winter, filling
detailed_description: A hearty choice for a long workday ahead. A steaming bowl of white sticky rice is drenched in thick braised pork sauce, served with melt-in-your-mouth braised pork belly and a piece of rich, nutty liver pâté.

name: Bò nướng chảo gang (Cast-Iron Pan Grilled Beef)
taste_profile: Fun sizzling sound, fragrant with butter, deeply savory sizzling-hot beef
ai_search_tags: gathering, casual drinking, sizzling, richly fatty, weekend, street food
detailed_description: A familiar, affordable grilled dish found on the old quarter's sidewalks. Ingredients are grilled directly on a cast-iron pan lined with foil and margarine, sizzling loudly and releasing an irresistible, mouthwatering aroma.

name: Lẩu ếch măng cay (Spicy Frog and Bamboo Shoot Hot Pot)
taste_profile: Fiery spicy from chili satay, crunchy and gently sour bamboo shoots, chewy sweet frog meat
ai_search_tags: gathering, dinner, fiery spicy, casual drinking, weekend, warming
detailed_description: A bubbling hot pot with a striking red hue from chili satay that's instantly eye-catching. Firm, plump frog legs are pre-stir-fried with sour, crunchy bamboo shoots, then simmered in the hot pot to soak up the spices, making for a perfect dish to share with friends.

name: Ngan cháy tỏi (Garlic-Crisped Muscovy Duck)
taste_profile: Tender sweet, deeply marinated duck meat, nutty crispy fried garlic
ai_search_tags: casual drinking, savory, fragrant, dinner, gathering, weekend
detailed_description: A relatively new dish that has quickly taken Hanoi's food scene by storm. Richly marinated duck pieces are fried until golden and charred at the edges, then piled high with dozens of whole crispy fried garlic cloves, fragrant without any bitterness.

name: Bánh mì (Vietnamese Sandwich)
taste_profile: Crispy baguette, savory pâté, rich meats, fresh and crunchy vegetables, balanced sweet-and-sour pickles
ai_search_tags: sandwich, breakfast, quick lunch, street food, budget-friendly, portable, iconic
detailed_description: Banh Mi is a world-famous Vietnamese sandwich featuring a crispy, airy baguette sliced open and smeared with rich pâté and mayonnaise, then stuffed with various meats, fresh cucumber, coriander, and sweet-and-sour pickled vegetables for a perfect harmony of textures and flavors.
